levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the energy flow description for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.
The module describes the top-level energy paths, conversion stages, distribution paths, thermal energy removal paths, monitoring points, and protection boundaries associated with ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Energy Flow Scope
levelledPara.para: The energy flow scope includes the controlled movement of energy from the aircraft-level energy source or conversion equipment through the hybrid-electric power architecture to the propulsion drive equipment and associated support functions.

This module is descriptive only. It does not define final load calculations, detailed wiring schedules, equipment ratings, protection settings, thermal sizing, or certified performance limits. Those values shall be controlled by the applicable engineering, safety, certification, and verification baselines.

levelledPara.title: Principal Energy Flow Paths
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture contains the following principal energy flow paths:

listItem.para: Primary energy source to energy conversion equipment.
Energy conversion equipment to electrical conditioning equipment.
Electrical conditioning equipment to distribution and protection equipment.
Distribution and protection equipment to propulsion drive equipment.
Propulsion drive equipment to electric machines or propulsive devices.
Electrical and conversion losses to thermal management equipment.
Thermal management equipment to heat rejection or redistribution interfaces.
Monitoring and diagnostic data flow to aircraft indication, recording, and maintenance systems.

levelledPara.title: Primary Energy Input
levelledPara.para: The primary energy input function supplies available energy to the hybrid-electric architecture in accordance with the applicable aircraft configuration. The energy source may be connected to conversion, conditioning, buffering, isolation, and protection equipment before energy is made available to the propulsion power path.
Energy input availability, source readiness, isolation state, and protective status shall be monitored and reported to the applicable control, indication, and maintenance functions.

levelledPara.title: Energy Conversion Flow
levelledPara.para: The energy conversion flow transforms source energy into controlled electrical power suitable for the hybrid-electric propulsion architecture. The conversion stage may include conversion equipment, control electronics, contactors, protection devices, sensors, and local cooling provisions.
Conversion flow shall remain within the operating limits defined by the approved system configuration, equipment qualification baseline, thermal capacity, and safety assessment evidence.

levelledPara.title: Electrical Conditioning Flow
levelledPara.para: The electrical conditioning flow stabilizes, regulates, or adapts electrical power before distribution to propulsion and associated hybrid-electric loads. This flow may include voltage control, current limitation, filtering, switching, isolation, and protection functions.
Conditioning functions shall support controlled response to transient demand, abnormal electrical conditions, degraded equipment availability, and commanded mode transitions.

levelledPara.title: Distribution and Protection Flow
levelledPara.para: The distribution and protection flow routes controlled electrical energy from the conditioning equipment to the propulsion drive equipment and applicable auxiliary hybrid-electric loads.
The distribution path shall include controlled switching, isolation, fault containment, protection coordination, status monitoring, and maintenance reporting provisions.

levelledPara.title: Propulsion Drive Energy Flow
levelledPara.para: The propulsion drive energy flow transfers controlled electrical power to motor drive equipment and electric machines. The drive equipment converts the electrical energy into commanded mechanical or propulsive output in accordance with aircraft-level propulsion demand and local equipment limits.
The propulsion drive energy flow shall be monitored for availability, commanded output, limitation state, protection status, and abnormal operating conditions.

levelledPara.title: Auxiliary and Buffer Energy Flow
levelledPara.para: Auxiliary and buffer energy flow supports transient management, equipment protection, control power supply, limited non-mission buffering, and safe transition between operating states where applicable to the approved configuration.
Buffer energy shall not be treated as the primary mission energy source unless explicitly authorized by the certified programme baseline.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Energy Flow
levelledPara.para: Thermal energy flow is generated by conversion losses, electrical distribution losses, drive equipment losses, control electronics, and associated auxiliary equipment.
The thermal management function collects, transports, rejects, or redistributes this thermal energy to maintain equipment operation within defined temperature limits.

levelledPara.title: Heat Rejection Flow
levelledPara.para: Heat rejection flow transfers thermal energy from hybrid-electric equipment to the applicable aircraft heat rejection or redistribution interfaces. The available heat rejection capacity may limit propulsion energy flow, equipment availability, or operating mode.
Reduced thermal capacity shall be reported to the control and maintenance functions and may cause limitation, degraded operation, or safe-state transition.

levelledPara.title: Energy Flow Monitoring
levelledPara.para: Energy flow monitoring provides controlled measurement or status reporting for source availability, conversion status, voltage, current, power, temperature, isolation state, protection state, and equipment health.
Monitoring data shall support flight crew indication, aircraft recording, central maintenance reporting, diagnostics, fault isolation, verification evidence, and lifecycle traceability.

levelledPara.title: Abnormal Energy Flow Conditions
levelledPara.para: Abnormal energy flow conditions include loss of source availability, conversion limitation, over-current, over-voltage, under-voltage, isolation fault, thermal exceedance, drive limitation, protection trip, communication loss, or degraded monitoring capability.
Abnormal conditions shall be detected, isolated, limited, reported, and managed in accordance with the approved control, safety, certification, and maintenance baselines.

levelledPara.title: Energy Flow Protection Philosophy
levelledPara.para: The protection philosophy shall prevent uncontrolled propagation of electrical, thermal, or control faults through the hybrid-electric energy path.
Protection functions shall support controlled isolation, load limitation, protective shutdown, safe-state transition, maintenance message generation, and traceable fault recording.

levelledPara.title: Operating Mode Effects on Energy Flow
levelledPara.para: The energy flow path changes according to operating mode. Normal mode permits controlled energy flow within nominal limits. Monitored mode increases supervision or reporting. Limited mode restricts available power or load allocation. Degraded mode maintains controlled operation with reduced function. Safe-state mode isolates or removes energy flow as required to preserve safety.

levelledPara.title: Energy Flow Boundaries
levelledPara.para: This data module describes energy flow at architecture level only. It does not allocate final power ratings, cable sizing, protection thresholds, cooling capacities, heat exchanger ratings, dispatch limits, or certified performance values.
Those values shall be defined in controlled engineering datasets, interface control records, verification plans, equipment qualification data, and certification evidence.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module supports deterministic traceability between architecture-level energy flow definition, hybrid-electric system interfaces, S1000D CSDB descriptive content, safety assessment evidence, verification evidence, maintenance reporting, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, energy flow baseline, interface control baseline, programme configuration baseline, safety assessment evidence, and verification records before CSDB release.
